column1: Dynamic logic = 4.
column2: 動態邏輯 = 4.

column1: Warning:\n\n"Leisure Suit Larry in the Land of the Lounge Lizards" contains some elements of plot which may not be  considered appropriate for some children.
column2: 警告：\n\n「休閒西裝賴瑞在鬼混蜥蜴樂園」包含某些可能不適合兒童的情節元素。

column1: How old are you?
column2: 你多大？

column1: To verify you are really %v209, please answer five simple questions.
column2: 為了確認你確實是 %v209，請回答五個簡單問題。

column1:
Sorry.

This game can only be played by adults, or with an adult.

Please find an adult, come back, and try again.
column2:
抱歉。

這個遊戲只能由成年人或與成年人一起玩。

請找一個成年人，回來，再試一次。

column1: Oops. You blew that one!\n\nIf you miss another, you can't possibly be %v209.
column2: 哎呀。你搞砸了那個！\n\n如果你再錯一個，你不可能是 %v209。

column1:
You're a kid!!

Shame on you for trying to deceive a friendly game like this!

Please play again when you're older.
column2:
你是小孩！！

你真丟臉，竟然想騙一個友善的遊戲！

等你長大再玩吧。

column1: Correct.
column2: 正確。

column1: Thank you.\n\nAnd now, slip into your leisure suit and prepare to enter the "Land of the Lounge Lizards" with "Leisure Suit Larry!"
column2: 謝謝你。\n\n現在，穿上你的休閒西裝，準備和「休閒西裝賴瑞」一起進入「鬼混蜥蜴樂園」！

column1: Warning!\n\nThis game may be hazardous to those with heart conditions!
column2: 警告！\n\n這個遊戲對心臟不好的人可能很危險！

column1: No, you're not!
column2: 不，你不是！

column1: Question #%v208. Correct answer is %v211.
column2: 問題 #%v208。正確答案是 %v211。

column1: %v62 cycles in %v63 seconds
column2: %v62 個循環耗時 %v63 秒

column1: Hey! You're not alive yet.
column2: 嘿！你還沒出生呢。

column1: The Sierra adventure team races to your rescue, dumping your worthless, leisure suit- clad hulk into their giant bit bucket, for recycling into future games. Their underground workshop is humming with activity. This is "Where Adventurers Are Made, Not Born!"
column2: 山塞拉冒險隊趕來救你，把你那穿著休閒西裝的無用軀殼丟進他們的大回收桶，用來為未來的遊戲回收利用。他們的地下工作室嗡嗡作響。這是「冒險家的製造廠，不是出生地！」

column1: Commander Cavin quickly assembles a replacement Larry, while subordinates repair players damaged in other Sierra adventure games.
column2: 卡文司令迅速組裝了一個替代賴瑞，而下屬則修復了在其他山塞拉冒險遊戲中受損的玩家。

column1: With your new body and ego, you are returned to fight again the battles of Lost Wages!\n\n(Now you know what we have to go through every time you press that "Restart" key.)
column2: 用你的新身體和自尊心，你被送回去再次與失樂城的戰鬥！\n\n（現在你知道每次你按下「重新開始」鍵時我們要經歷什麼了。）

column1: Larry, when are you gonna learn to stay out of those dark alleys!!
column2: 賴瑞，你什麼時候才能學會不進那些黑暗小巷！！

column1: Hey guys!! He's screwed up again! Dump this one down the tubes, and get him another body!
column2: 「嘿，傢伙們！！他又搞砸了！把這個沖進下水道，給他換個身體！」

column1: Since you're dead, why are you talking?? Just hang on for the ride.
column2: 既然你已經死了，為什麼還在說話？？只管享受這趟旅程吧。

column1: It appears he wants something -- YOU! Get out of here!
column2: 看起來他想要什麼——你！滾出去！

column1: The cabbie snarls a "Where to, buddy?" as he swings the meter flag down.
column2: 計程車司機惡狠狠地說：「去哪兒，老兄？」同時按下計程表。

column1: Lookin' for action, eh? Dis town is full of it. We gotta disco that's usually full of foxes this time of night. Den dere's the casino, next to the all-night weddin' chapel. And we gotta lovely bar, but you've been dere. Me, I like da convenience store next to da disco, but of course, that's just personal preference.
column2: 「尋求刺激是吧？這鎮上多得是。咱們有間舞廳，這鐘點通常美女滿滿。然後啊，還有賭場，就在全天候禮拜堂隔壁。咱還有間不錯的酒吧，你去過啦。我個人嘛，比較喜歡舞廳旁的便利店，當然啦，這只是個人偏好。」

column1: It won't take us long to get back dere, but I'll have to circle 'round da block.
column2: 「回那邊不會花太久，不過我得繞過街區。」

column1: Well, here we are, buddy. That'll be $%v200.00.
column2: 「好啦，到了老兄。該給 $%v200.00。」

column1: So, wadda we gonna do, buddy? Ya just gonna sit here all night? I gotta earn a livin' too, ya know!
column2: 「那怎樣啦，老兄？你要在這坐整晚嗎？我也得賺口飯吃啊！」

column1: Why don'cha wait'll we git there, buddy?
column2: 「為啥不等咱們到那兒再說，老兄？」

column1: Sorry, but the meter's runnin'. Where ja wanna go?
column2: 「抱歉，計程表在跑。你要去哪兒？」

column1: Thanks, buddy. Maybe I'll see ya again sometime!
column2: 「謝了，老兄。也許哪天還會見面！」

column1: Waddaya tryin' to do, peahead! You're not gonna stiff me, and get away with it!
column2: 「你在搞什麼呢，傻瓜！別想賴帳溜掉！」

column1: You wish you hadn't. Mr. America, he ain't. His strongest feature seems to be his odor!
column2: 你會後悔看他。美國先生他不是。他最突出的特徵好像是他的臭味！

column1: He wonders why you keep looking at him, and flashes a coy little smile back at you in the mirror. Your stomach turns, and not from the ride!
column2: 他納悶你為啥一直看他，在鏡子裡回給你個嬌羞的微笑。你的胃翻江倒海，不是暈車！

column1: The driver looks at you and says, "Hey! What's that you got? Wine?" He grabs your bottle of wine, and guzzles it down!
column2: 司機看著你說：「嘿！你拿的啥？紅酒？」他一把搶過你的酒瓶，猛灌下肚！

column1: Ok, but it'll cost ja extra!
column2: 「好吧，不過要加錢！」

column1: Waddaya mean, 'no money!!' snarls the driver. "Step outside my office, chump!"
column2: 「什麼叫『沒錢！！』」司機怒吼。「滾出我的車，傻瓜！」

column1: Ok. You got it, Mac!
column2: 「好啦，沒問題，老兄！」

column1: Hang on, Larry. He's swerving all over the road!
column2: 抓好，Larry。他在路上大搖大擺！

column1: Whew! You're glad to get out of here.
column2: 呼！你很高興終於逃出來了。

column1: It seems a bridge abutment has brought your game to a sudden stop! Remember, Larry: "Friends don't let cabbies drive drunk!"
column2: 好像橋墩讓你的遊戲戛然而止了！記住啊 Larry：「真朋友不會讓計程車司機酒駕！」

column1: I'm sorry, but it's against my personal philosophy to accept a gratuity!
column2: 「抱歉，不過收小費違背我個人哲學！」

column1: Sheepishly, you apologize to the cabbie for not carrying enough money to pay for the ride.
column2: 你尷尬地向計程車司機道歉，因為沒帶足夠的錢付車資。

column1: So? Are ya gonna get outta my cab, or not?? Thanks for da business, but I didn't want ta make no career outta this!
column2: 「怎樣？你要滾出我的車不？謝謝你的生意，不過我不想靠這個當飯碗！」

column1: So? Are ya gonna pay up, or do I have ta get rough with cha??
column2: 「怎樣？你要付錢，還是要我動粗？」

column1: It seems to be running a little fast, but then, this is an expensive town!
column2: 好像跑得有點快，不過嘛，這是個高消費的城鎮！

column1: You wonder if cab companies have full-time employees to make these floors sticky.
column2: 你納悶計程車公司是否有專職員工讓地板黏黏的。

column1: Even the ceiling's grungy.
column2: 連天花板都髒兮兮的。

column1: Ok, buddy, says the cabbie. "But why would cha wanna go there?"
column2: 「好啦，老兄，」司機說。「不過你幹嘛想去那兒？」

column1: He sure drives fast, doesn't he?
column2: 他開得真快，不是嗎？

column1: Nope. Next time, make up your mind before ya gets in da cab!
column2: 「不行。下次上車前先決定好！」

column1: You are outside Lefty's bar. Isn't it wonderful what can be done with a little neon?
column2: 你在 Lefty 酒吧外。霓虹燈的魔力真妙不可言，不是嗎？

column1: There is a sign on that utility pole.
column2: 那根電線桿上有個招牌。

column1: It appears that the hooker gave you a little more than you bargained for!
column2: 看起來那個妓女給你的比預期多得多！

column1: Considering the looks of this place, you decide that next time you'd be better off to spend a little more money on your computer games!
column2: 看這地方的樣子，你決定下次花多點錢在電玩上會比較划算！

column1: While life may be possible, it is no longer worth living.
column2: 雖然還能活著，但人生已不值得活。

column1: The windows are too dirty to see through.
column2: 窗戶太髒了，根本透不了光。

column1: Nah. Lefty would probably break both your legs!
column2: 別啦。Lefty 大概會砸斷你兩條腿！

column1: Nice tuck and roll job, eh?
column2: 不錯的翻滾停靠啊，嗯？

column1: Welcome
column2: Welcome

column1: Amazed by your own cleverness, you lift the door mat and discover...
column2: 為自己的聰慧驚喜，你抬起門墊，發現……

column1: ...absolutely nothing!
column2: ……根本沒有東西！

column1: (What did you expect? A key?)
column2: （你期望啥？鑰匙？）

column1: You can't. The door is securely locked.
column2: 不行。門牢牢鎖著。

column1: Lefty's closes at 3:00 a.m.
column2: Lefty 的營業時間到凌晨 3 點。

column1: STOP, PERVERT!!! shouts the cop.\n\n"I wonder who he's yelling at," you think.
column2: 「住手，色鬼！！！」警察大喊。\n\n「我不知道他在吼誰，」你想。

column1: Caught you, pervert! the cop shouts. "We don't like people exposing themselves around this town, especially wearing one of those!"
column2: 「抓到你了，色鬼！」警察喝道。「我們這鎮上不喜歡有人亂露，尤其穿著那種東西！」

column1: Next time, if you insist on wearing your "lubber" in public, you should at least zip your fly.
column2: 下次，你堅持在公開場合穿著那種「乳膠褲」，至少要拉上拉鍊。

column1: He appears to be chasing someone!
column2: 他好像在追什麼人！

column1: Try moving to the east end of the fire escape.
column2: 試試移動到逃生梯的東邊。

column1: Try moving to the west end of the fire escape.
column2: 試試移動到逃生梯的西邊。

column1: %m7 and a fence.
column2: %m7 和一道柵欄。

column1: Nah. That room needs the fresh air!
column2: 算了。那房間需要透透氣！

column1: You are on a fire escape. A rusty ladder is at one end. East of the fire escape is another window, just out of your reach.
column2: 你站在逃生梯上。一端有條生鏽的梯子。逃生梯東邊是另一扇窗，剛好搆不著。

column1: %m7, a fence, and something sitting in the east window.
column2: %m7、一道柵欄，還有東邊窗裡坐著的東西。

column1: It's a nice little alley, with a trash bin, two windows, a fire escape
column2: 這是條不錯的小巷，有垃圾桶、兩扇窗、一台逃生梯

column1: Move closer to the ladder.
column2: 靠近梯子。

column1: (Of course, you can't play the violin now, but you get the idea.)
column2: （當然啦，你現在拉不了小提琴，但你懂意思。）

column1: Unlock it? From the outside?
column2: 開鎖？從外面？

column1: You can't. It's locked from the inside.
column2: 不行。它從裡面上了鎖。

column1: Looking inside the west window, you see the room you just left.
column2: 看著西邊窗戶內，你看到剛才離開的房間。

column1: Looking at the east window, you notice a bottle of pills sitting on the window ledge.
column2: 看著東邊窗戶，你注意到窗台上放著瓶藥丸。

column1: There are two windows high above you. The west window by the fire escape has a dim, reddish glow; the east window is dark. There may be something on the sill of the east window, but you can't make it out from down here.
column2: 上面遠處有兩扇窗。逃生梯旁的西邊窗發著暗紅色微光；東邊窗一片黑暗。東邊窗台上可能有東西，但下面看不清楚。

column1: You broke it, didn't you?
column2: 你打破它了，不是嗎？

column1: You lean far out over the railing before remembering that agility is yet another of your short suits.
column2: 你身體傾出欄杆，才想起靈敏度也是你的短處。

column1: With the rope securely holding you to the railing, you can reach all the way over to the window. NOW, of all times, you notice the window's locked!
column2: 繩子把你牢牢綁在欄杆上，你可以伸過去碰到窗戶。現在、偏偏現在，你才發現窗子鎖著！

column1: Subtly, you smash the window with your hammer. Your future as a second-story man seems quite plausible!
column2: 小心翼翼，你用榔頭砸碎窗戶。你當竊賊的前景真是光輝璀璨！

column1: You got 'em! But what are they?
column2: 你搞定了！可是那是啥？

column1: You might try reaching over to the window first.
column2: 你可以先試試伸手去那扇窗。

column1: You consider sticking your hand through a solid glass window, but realize that if you did, you wouldn't be able to play the violin.
column2: 你考慮把手穿過玻璃窗，但想到這樣的話你就拉不了小提琴了。

column1: It already is.
column2: 它已經是了。

column1: What is this; a death wish?
column2: 這是啥；死願嗎？

column1: You already took them. Why do this again?
column2: 你已經拿了。為啥又要來一遍？

column1: Amidst assorted, odoriferous refuse from Lefty's clientel, you discover Lefty's old, left-handed hammer.
column2: 在 Lefty 客人的各種臭烘烘垃圾中，你發現了 Lefty 的老舊左手榔頭。

column1: There's nothing else here worth describing, let alone taking.
column2: 這裡沒別的東西值得一提，更別說拿了。

column1: YUCK!!
Unfortunately, you can't see much from out here.
column2: 可惜的是，你從這兒看不了太多東西。

column1: Wow! Does your head hurt!!
column2: 哇！你的頭好痛！！

column1: Hey! Where are you?
column2: 嘿！你在哪兒？

column1: After a careful analysis of your surroundings, you conclude you are at the bottom of a pile of garbage!
column2: 經過仔細分析周遭，你結論是你在一堆垃圾的底部！

column1: Move to the front of the trash bin.
column2: 移動到垃圾桶前面。

column1: There's no way to reach the ladder from down here. It's only accessible from above.
column2: 從下面沒辦法碰到梯子。只能從上面進去。

column1: You see alleys stretching off to infinity.
column2: 你看見小巷一直延伸到無窮遠。

column1: The fence is much too tall for you to climb over.
column2: 柵欄對你來說太高了，爬不過去。

column1: It's not long enough to reach from here.
column2: 它長度不夠，搆不到這裡。

column1: It's not long enough to reach anywhere, but you might want to use it for safety purposes.
column2: 它長度根本不夠搆到任何地方，不過你可以拿它當安全用途。

column1:
HOUSE RULES

This table has a $20.00 limit. Bets must be a multiple of $2.00.

Dealer must hit 16, and must stand on 17.

Blackjack pays 1-1/2 to 1 odds.

Any six cards under 22 wins.
column2:
賭場規則

這桌最高限額 20 元。賭注必須是 2 元的倍數。

莊家 16 點要補牌，17 點要停。

二十一點賠 1.5 比 1。

六張牌且不超過 21 點就贏。

column1:
Insurance bets are offered whenever the dealer shows an ace. They are half your bet, and pay 2 to 1 odds.

You may split a pair or surrender whenever you have only two cards.

You may double down if your cards total 10 or 11 points.

column2: 看來她不喜歡那樣！

column1: She wouldn't like that.
column2: 她不會喜歡那樣。

column1: Say; what's this? A beautiful woman all alone in a hot tub! And, she's smiling at you? And, and...
column2: 咦；這是什麼？一個美女獨自一人在溫泉！而且，她在對你微笑？而且，而且…

column1: Why take it, when you can drink it?
column2: 何必拿著它，當你可以喝它？

column1: It looks like a bottle of fine champagne, suitable for two.
column2: 看起來像一瓶上好香檳，適合兩人。

column1: They ARE off.
column2: 它們是關著的。

column1: They ARE on.
column2: 它們是開著的。

column1: Her gold bracelet is engraved with the word "Eve."
column2: 她的金色手鐲上刻著「Eve」。

column1: ...and, she speaks, "Hi, handsome. Where have you been all my life?"
column2: …然後，她說話，「嗨，帥哥。你這輩子去哪了？」

column1: (Larry, this one seems to LIKE you!)
column2: （Larry，這個似乎喜歡你！）

column1: Oooh, I like that name!
column2: 「喔，我喜歡那個名字！」

column1: This is the living room of the hotel's penthouse suite. Obviously, the hotel spared no expense here. There's a fireplace on the wall to the south. No one is here, but you hear a gurgling noise coming from somewhere.
column2: 這是旅館頂樓套房的客廳。顯然飯店在這裡沒有省錢。南邊的牆上有壁爐。這裡沒人，但你聽到咕嚕咕嚕的聲音。

column1: You wonder how they can make those gas logs look so real!
column2: 你想知道他們怎麼把那些瓦斯木柴搞得這麼逼真！

column1: There is a button on either side of the elevator.
column2: 電梯兩側各有一個按鈕。

column1: The bear looks back at you with a glassy-eyed stare, possibly because his eyes are made of glass!
column2: 熊用呆滯的眼神看著你，可能因為他的眼睛是玻璃製的！

column1: It's another one of those boring works of modern art; this one is signed "Mark Crowe."
column2: 又是一件無聊的現代藝術品；這個簽名是「Mark Crowe」。

column1: Yep. It's a sofa!
column2: 是的。那是沙發！

column1: From this height, the view of the city is spectacular.
column2: 從這個高度，城市的景色棒極了。

column1: The elevator doors are already open. Just walk inside.
column2: 電梯門已經開了。直接走進去。

column1: Instead of sitting, you should investigate that gurgling sound you hear.
column2: 與其坐著，你應該調查那個咕嚕咕嚕的聲音。

column1: There's a sound coming from outside.
column2: 有聲音從外面傳來。

column1: The door to the terrace is already open.
column2: 露台的門已經開了。

column1: You find nothing but dust.
column2: 你只找到灰塵。

column1: Tired already?
column2: 已經累了？

column1: You can't. It's screwed to the wall.
column2: 你不行。它被螺釘釘在牆上。

column1: The penthouse bedroom is quite deluxe.
column2: 頂樓臥室相當豪華。

column1: Didn't your Mother tell you that doing that would make you go blind?
column2: 妳媽沒告訴你這樣做會瞎嗎？

column1: Geez, Larry. Do we have to?
column2: 天啊，Larry。我們非得嗎？

column1: Now?! When she's waiting for you on the bed??
column2: 現在？！當她在床上等你？？

column1: It is open.
column2: 它是開的。

column1: It's not open.
column2: 它沒開。

column1: There's no inflatable doll out here.
column2: 這裡沒有充氣娃娃。

column1: All right, you asked for it.\n\nIn fact, you've asked for it twice! Here we go...
column2: 好吧，你自找的。\n\n實際上，你要過兩次！我們開始…

column1: Got her!
column2: 得到她了！

column1: Selecting your own personal favorite from the three available openings, you shyly try out the doll. This has to be the kinkiest thing you've done in a long time, Larry.\n\nYou gradually increase your tempo as you lose your inhibitions (not to mention your self-esteem). Faster and faster you go, until suddenly, there's a loud, flatulent sound...
column2: 從三個開口中選擇你最喜歡的，你害羞地試試娃娃。這一定是你很久以來做過最怪的事，Larry。\n\n當你失去理性（更別說自尊心），你逐漸加速。越來越快，直到突然，有一聲響亮的放屁聲…

column1: You lost her, remember!
column2: 你失去她了，記得嗎！

column1: You've found a closet full of uninteresting stuff, plus one inflatable doll.
column2: 你發現一個衣櫃滿是無趣的東西，加上一個充氣娃娃。

column1: The rest of the closet is rather dull compared to the inflatable doll.
column2: 衣櫃的其他部分和充氣娃娃比相當無趣。

column1: Walking out of the closet, you clumsily catch your new friend on a protruding nail! There's a loud, flatulent sound, and...
column2: 走出衣櫃，你笨拙地把新朋友掛在突出的釘子上！有一聲響亮的放屁聲…

column1: The closet is closed.
column2: 衣櫃關了。

column1: You huff, and you puff, and you blow your doll up. Holding her out at arm's length, you pause to admire your handiwork...
column2: 你吹呀，你吹呀，你把娃娃吹起來。把她舉到手臂長度外，你停下來欣賞你的傑作…

column1: ...somehow you expected more.
column2: …不知為何你期待更多。

column1: What more would you want to see?
column2: 你還想看什麼？

column1: Move out of the way of the door first.
column2: 先移開門的方向。

column1: Go get her, Larry.
column2: 去得到她，Larry。

column1: The bedspread is made of genuine, 100 per cent, acrylic zebrahide.
column2: 床罩由真正百分百的亞克力斑馬皮製成。

column1:
Congratulations, Larry!!!

You've done it!!

You've successfully completed your evening in Sierra World, lost your virginity, and emerged victorious!
column2:
恭喜，Larry！！！

你做到了！！

你成功完成了在 Sierra World 的晚上，失去了童貞，並獲得勝利！

column1: Of course, your feelings of success are short-lived, since this is where Ken Williams has to tell you about our soon-to-be-released sequel.\n\nHey, Ken! Come here, and give 'em your pitch...
column2: 當然，你的勝利快感短暫得很，因為這裡非得讓肯威廉斯跟你聊聊我們即將推出的續作。\n\n喂，肯！過來，給他們推銷一下…

column1: Hi, folks! Remember, coming to this very computer Real Soon Now, is the next installment in the saga of Larry Laffer, traveling software salesman. I'd tell you the name now, but we haven't made one up yet!\n\n(Be sure to pick up a few extra copies, just in case our disk copier is screwed up again!)
column2: 嗨，各位！記住，很快就要在這部電腦上推出 Larry Laffer 旅行推銷員傳奇的下一章了。我很想現在就告訴你片名，可我們還沒想好呢！\n\n(最好多買幾份，免得我們的軟碟複製機又出問題!)

column1: Oh, wait. We almost forget to tell you how well you played.
column2: 噢，等等。我們差點忘了評評你玩得怎樣。

column1: You can't bear the thought of deflating your new friend, so you don't!
column2: 你捨不得戳破你新交的朋友，所以作罷。

column1: What fun is an uninflated inflatable doll?
column2: 充氣娃娃沒氣，還有什麼意思？

column1: ...thanks for playing, and be sure to tell each and every one of your friends to buy their own personal copy of this game.
column2: …感謝遊玩，別忘了跟你每一個朋友吹捧這款遊戲，叫他們去買。

column1: Move inside the closet.
column2: 鑽進衣櫃裡。

column1: You played ok, I guess!
column2: 嘛，你玩得還可以啦！

column1: On behalf of Mark and Sandy, Al and Marg, Jeff and Chris, Russ and Kim, Larry and Bob, Ken and Roberta, Bob and Marilyn and/or Bob and Jeannine, John and Mary, Bob and Carol and Ted and Alice...
column2: 代表 Mark 和 Sandy、Al 和 Marg、Jeff 和 Chris、Russ 和 Kim、Larry 和 Bob、Ken 和 Roberta、Bob 和 Marilyn 或/和 Bob 和 Jeannine、John 和 Mary、Bob 和 Carol 和 Ted 和 Alice…

column1: Don't you find it difficult to have a meaningful relationship with latex?
column2: 你不覺得跟橡膠娃娃建立有意義的關係很難嗎？

column1: OK.
column2: OK.

column1: Love ya, baby!
column2: 寶貝，愛死你了！

column1: Sorry. One's your limit!
column2: 抱歉。只有一個是你的極限！

column1: You don't have time to rest!
column2: 你沒有時間休息！

column1: Perhaps if you first established mouth/latex contact...
column2: 說不定先來個嘴對橡膠的接觸…

column1: Picking up the corner of the expensive, vinyl, zebra-skin bedspread, you discover...
column2: 你掀起那張昂貴的乙烯基斑馬紋床罩一角，發現…

column1: ...nothing.
column2: …什麼都沒有。

column1: That's not what she's for, Larry.
column2: 那不是她的用途，Larry。

column1: ...and that's exactly the way she used to look!
column2: …這正是她從前的樣子！

column1: Ladies and gentlemen...\n\nMr. Ken Williams!
column2: 女士們、先生們…\n\n肯威廉斯先生！

column1: YO!!\n\nTAXI!!!
column2: 「喲！！\n\n計程車！！！」

column1: Vowing to hold your breath forever, you enter this miserable excuse for public transportation.
column2: 你發誓要忍著呼吸，\n踏進這台破得不像話的大眾運輸。

column1: It's not very clean.
column2: 它不太乾淨。

column1: It's not very new.
column2: 它不太新。

column1: (And those are its good points!)
column2: （這還是它的優點呢！）

column1: Look out! Here he comes again!
column2: 小心！他又來了！

column1: Obviously, he thinks you're a fire plug.
column2: 顯然他把你當成消防栓。

column1: Dogs like that make you want to keep moving.
column2: 這種狗讓你只想快點走。

column1: I can't wait all day, bub!
column2: 「我沒時間等一整天，老兄！」

column1: You'll not ride with me again!
column2: 「你別想再上我的車！」

column1: Sick-o!
column2: 噁心鬼！

column1: After all he's done for you?
column2: 他為你做了那麼多？

column1: He presently smells quite bad, although still better than you.
column2: 他現在臭得要死，\n不過還是比你好聞。

column1: TAXI\nSTAND
column2: 「計程車\n停靠站」

column1: It's dark. Real dark.
column2: 黑漆漆的。真的很黑。

column1: You'd better get out of the street first.
column2: 你最好先離開街道。

column1: BEEP\nBEEP!
Thousands of cars stream past, just below the edge of your screen.
column2: 數千輛汽車呼嘯而過，\n就在你螢幕邊緣下方。

column1: Trust me, they're there!
column2: 相信我，它們就在那兒！

column1: It smells like an old cab.
column2: 聞起來像老計程車。

column1: Isn't it funny how there's so little traffic?
column2: 奇怪啦，怎麼交通這麼少？

column1: The glow of the city lights obliterate the stars.
column2: 城市燈光的光芒淹沒了星星。

column1: As you bend over to do so, his bare teeth convince you otherwise.
column2: 當你彎下腰時，\n他露出的牙齒讓你改變主意。

column1: You shinny up the pole, read the sign, and come right back down.
column2: 你爬上竿子，讀了標誌，\n然後馬上爬下來。

column1: Who'd want him?
column2: 誰要他啊？

column1: There is a sign on the pole.
column2: 竿子上有個標誌。
